steps.title: Identify the clause structure
steps.content: Before the blank, we have an independent clause (“The clashing notes can echo a long way across the mountains”) followed by “which is why” — a relative clause that introduces a causal explanation. The blank must contain a clause that provides the logical reason.

steps.title: Apply the relative clause rule
steps.content: A relative clause introduced by “which is why” must use normal word order (subject before auxiliary verb) and end with a period that closes the sentence. Option B (“ganja has been used as a communication method.”) correctly applies this rule with normal word order and appropriate closing punctuation. Options A, C, and D violate this rule by either misplacing question marks within the relative clause (A, D) or inverting the word order unnecessarily (C). That matches choice B — B is correct.
